course_description:
In recent times, particularly in Australia, there has been an emphasis on athlete pathways including coaching and player development frameworks. This course recognises the challenges that confront coaches and pathway managers in negotiating the journey around developing elite athletes.

The course focuses on two key areas, firstly the complexity in implementing talent development programs and some implications around long term athlete development. Secondly, the course focuses on the role of the coach as a key driver in designing 'optimal' environments intended to support athlete development. This includes, but is not de-limited to the integration of contemporary pedagogical frameworks intended to support athlete skill development. Both of these key areas, in this course, are driven by the ecological meta-theory.

The course aims to enhance the coaches understanding of the issues associated with talent and its ensuing components, as well as develop the coaches’ skills in navigating the difficulties of athlete development across both able-bodied and Paralympic programs. Finally capturing the talent development environment and its application to the coach's practice is explored across to case reports.ﾠ

prerequisites: We recommend completing the following courses before enrolling in this one:

course_aims: To offer students with an insight to the complex, dynamic and multifaceted nature of talent and its subsequent development across the high-performance sporting continuum. To do so, this course will touch on a variety of aspects of athletic performance, inclusive of but not de-limited to, skill learning, physiological responses to training, and athletic movement quality. Students will leave this course with an appreciation of how to design ‘optimal’ learning environments intended to support key aspects of athletic performance.

learning_outcomes.description: understand the general principles of talent identification and development including long term athlete development models
understand the role of a range of sub-disciplines within the high performance support team or program including sports science and specialist coaching
be able to interpret physiological; biomechanical and psychological test data to assess the strengths and weaknesses of an athlete
develop ‘optimal’ learning environments to support and accelerate athlete development by drawing upon contemporary views of athlete performance
design and implement training programs using contemporary pedagogical frameworks that positively implicate aspects of athletic performance within an elite or developmental training environment

assessment_summary.due_date: 9/03/2026 - 13/03/2026; 30/03/2026 - 2/04/2026; Quizzes will open at 9:00 am Monday and must be completed by 5:00 pm Friday in Week’s 3 and 6. Students can complete the quiz anytime during the opening window, but once open will only have with 20-minutes for completion.

assessment_details.task_description: At the end of Module’s 1 and 2 there will be a 10 question MCQ – all questions originating from lecture and reading material. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Discretionary extensions are not available for this task.
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Case Report 1
assessment_details.task_description: Following the completion of Module 1, you will be required to outline the design of an ‘optimal’ athlete development environment in a sport of your choice, drawing upon the tenets of an ecological dynamics approach. This case report will consist of six main sections, with more details being provided on the task description located on the course Blackboard site. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 3 (23 Feb - 15 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Module 1 - Talent (Weeks 1 – 3) Lecture 1 | An overview to talent and its subsequent components Lecture 2 | An ecological approach to developing talent in sport Activity 3 | Readings and Multiple-Choice Quiz 1 Learning outcomes: L01, L02

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 4 To Week 6 (16 Mar - 05 Apr)
learning_activities.topic: Module 2 - Development by design (Weeks 4 – 6) Lecture 1 | Integrating pedagogical frameworks to support athlete development: The case of skill adaptability Lecture 2 | Developing the elite athlete: Physical performance considerations for coaches Activity 3 | Readings and Multiple-Choice Quiz 2 Learning outcomes: L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 7 To Week 9 (13 Apr - 03 May)
learning_activities.topic: Module 3 - Developing the Paralympic athlete (Weeks 7 – 9) Lecture 1 | The Paralympic pathway: Opportunities for the next generation of Paralympic athletes Lecture 2 | Clinical considerations for coaches: The case of wheelchair rugby Activity 3 | Readings Learning outcomes: L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 10 To Week 12 (04 May - 24 May)
learning_activities.topic: Module 4 - Case examples (Weeks 10 – 12) Case example 1 | Developing skill adaptability in Rugby Union: A discussion with Jim McKay Case example 2 | Holistic athlete development in Australian football: A discussion with Ian McKeown Activity 3 | Readings Learning outcomes: L05
